topic.title: Introduction to VlppParser2
topic.p: VlppParser2 provides GLR parser generator for C++, with generated XML and JSON parsers.

topic.title: Using Generated XML Parser
topic.p:
All functions and types are defined in vl::glr::xml. Before using the XML parser, you need to constructor a Parser object. Parser can be used parallelly with multiple XmlParseDocument or XmlParseElement calls. These two functions return Ptr<XmlDocument> and Ptr<XmlElement> respectively, and you can access the content in XML directly or by calling the following shortcut functions:
XmlPrint: Print an XML node to a writer. You can call it with stream::GenerateToStream to get a string from it, or call XmlToString to get the string directly.
XmlPrintContent: Print an all contents in an element to a writer. You can call it with stream::GenerateToStream to get a string from it, or call XmlGetValue to get the string directly.
XmlGetAttribute: Find an attribute in an element.
XmlGetElement: Find the first sub element in an element given the element name.
XmlGetElements: Find all sub elements in an element given the element name.
The predefined printing functions print XML in a compact format without indentation or new lines.
XmlNode::IVisitor is recommended to use for any recursive operations on an XML node. Parser and XmlNode::IVisitor follow the same design described in Generated APIs.

topic.title: Using Generated JSON Parser
topic.p:
All functions and types are defined in vl::glr::json. Parser can be used parallelly with multiple parsing calls to Parsing::ParseJRoot, which returns a Ptr<JsonNode>, and you can access the content in JSON directly or by calling the following shortcut functions:
JsonPrint: Print a JSON node to a writer. You can call it with stream::GenerateToStream to get a string from it, or call JsonToString to get the string directly.
You can pass an optional JsonFormatting to JsonPrint or JsonToString to control the formatting.
JsonNode::IVisitor is recommended to use for any recursive operations on a JSON node. Parser and JsonNode::IVisitor follow the same design described in Generated APIs.

topic.title: Building New Parsers
topic.p: A parser class and some IVisitor interfaces will be generated from GlrParserGen. The generated parser and all IVisitor interfaces follow the same design described in Generated APIs.

GlrParserGen in released tools can be used to generate C++ code starting from a Parser.xml. Take the remote protocol parser as an example:
<Parser name="GuiRemoteProtocol"> <Includes><VlppGlrParser.h></Includes> <CppNamespace>vl::presentation::remoteprotocol</CppNamespace> <HeaderGuard>VCZH_PRESENTATION_REMOTEPROTOCOL</HeaderGuard> <OutputDir>../Generated</OutputDir>
<Asts> <Ast name="Ast"> <CppNamespace>vl::presentation::remoteprotocol</CppNamespace> <ReflectionNamespace>presentation::remoteprotocol</ReflectionNamespace> <ClassPrefix>GuiRp</ClassPrefix> <BlockedUtilities> <Empty/> <Copy/> <Traverse/> <Builder/> </BlockedUtilities> <File file="Ast.txt"/> </Ast> </Asts>
<Lexer file="Lexer.txt"/> <Syntax name="Parser"> <File file="Syntax.txt"/> </Syntax> </Parser>
Those name attributes affect naming of generated files. /Parser/Syntax/@name will also become the name of the generated parser class. Here are some important elements:
/Parser/Includes: additional headers to include in generated files.
/Parser/CppNamespace: C++ namespace for generated classes.
/Parser/HeaderGuard: header guard for generated header files.
/Parser/OutputDir: output directory for generated files.
/Parser/Asts/Ast/CppNamespace: C++ namespace for all classes in this AST group.
/Parser/Asts/Ast/ReflectionNamespace: namespaces when appearing in reflection metadata.
/Parser/Asts/Ast/ClassPrefix: prefix for all class names in this AST group.
/Parser/Asts/Ast/BlockedUtilities: disable generation of certain utility classes for this AST group.
There can be multiple groups of ASTs, in each group there can be multiple AST definition files. AST classes are selectively visible to other AST files. Usually we only define one group.
There can be only one lexer file.
There can be multiple syntax files. Parser rules are selectively visible to other syntax files.
